full_text: <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> I appreciate the angel's question specifically in the King James Version.<|9.14|> <|10.32|> Ye men of Galilee, why stand ye gazing up into heaven?<|17.50|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> This question is not dissimilar from one of my favorite theological quips.<|7.00|> <|7.00|> Don't be so heavenly minded that you're no earthly good.<|13.00|> <|13.00|> The apostles preoccupied with a Jesus who has just ascended in their sight.<|22.00|> <|22.00|> A Jesus whose life, death, and resurrection had unfolded before them.<|28.80|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> They are distracted by the grandeur of the moment and cannot seem to redirect their gaze away from where Jesus was to where Jesus is.<|14.66|> <|16.00|> This is relatable.<|17.40|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> It is easy for much of life to be spent fixated on what was or what will be to the neglect of what is.<|13.86|> <|15.86|> Why stand ye gazing?<|17.90|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> is a question for every person, community, and nation<|5.00|> <|5.94|> that cannot, for whatever reason, move forward<|9.64|> <|9.64|> from that episode, that rupture, that deception,<|14.52|> <|14.52|> that now brings comfort and consolation.<|19.90|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Why stand ye gazing is a question for those who are in need of God's liberating Holy Spirit<|9.00|> <|9.00|> to set them free from the self-destruction, self-doubt, and self-sabotage inside of them.<|18.00|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Why stand ye gazing is a question for governments and others frozen into inaction on the climate<|9.62|> <|9.62|> crisis and other pressing social challenges of our and future generations' times.<|18.60|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Why stand ye gazing is a question for the person who needs to make that call, take that step, open that door into God's future.<|14.34|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Who tonight is gazing up into heaven to distract from the urgency of the earth before you?<|13.06|> <|15.36|> The same Christ who ascended to God's right hand.<|19.94|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> The same Christ is available to you and the whole creation, unburdened by the limitations of time and space.<|10.78|> <|11.18|> It is this Christ who is in union avec you in bread and wine, in prayer, in prayer, in prayer, in peace, in peace.<|19.20|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> in the mystery of the impoverished and oppressed,<|5.04|> <|6.42|> who whispers, whatever you need to face,<|10.80|> <|12.02|> I will face it with you.<|14.84|> <|16.30|> Amen.<|17.22|>
